reason: Could not construct a file-grounded refutation; the change is verified-correct. (1) No under-flagging risk: dependency is an advisory agent (block_threshold: none, fp_max 1.00), so capping output at low and opting out of medium loses zero blocking signal. The bad fixture (bad/unpinned-risky-dep) is untouched, so no known-bad slips through. (2) The good-fixture fix is demanded by the baseline evidence, not a defect being shipped. The proposal admits good/pinned-reputable-dep is NOT cleanly clean and fixes it. I verified the template ships httpx>=0.28 with NO upper cap (src/framework_cli/template/pyproject.toml.jinja lines 27 and 34); the fixture's httpx>=0.27,<1.0 diverges BELOW the floor (0.27<0.28) and adds a non-template cap. All three baseline repeats (findings/dependency/good/pinned-reputable-dep__r0/r1/r2.json) correctly flagged exactly this floor-mismatch and cap, so the reviewers were right and the fixture was the defect. (3) The fix (httpx>=0.28, drop the cap) makes the fixture byte-aligned to the template floor, so it becomes unambiguously clean and yields [] -- it does NOT make the good case dirty or the bad case ambiguous. (4) The prompt clauses (manifest-diff-only scope, no fabricated CVE ids, generic pin-floor phrasing) are consistent with the finalized rubric's grounding and scope-discipline sections and address the real over-reach seen in r0 (the speculative 'is httpx test-only / runtime?' reasoning about call sites the manifest diff cannot show). Ship it.
